verses.latin_vulgate: Ad filios Ammon.   Hæc dicit Dominus :  Numquid non filii sunt Israël,  aut hæres non est ei ?  cur igitur hæreditate possedit Melchom Gad,  et populus ejus in urbibus ejus habitavit ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לִבְנֵ֣י עַמּ֗וֹן כֹּ֚ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֔ה הֲבָנִ֥ים אֵין֙ לְיִשְׂרָאֵ֔ל אִם־יוֹרֵ֖שׁ אֵ֣ין ל֑וֹ מַדּ֗וּעַ יָרַ֤שׁ מַלְכָּם֙ אֶת־גָּ֔ד וְעַמּ֖וֹ בְּעָרָ֥יו יָשָֽׁב׃
verses.king_james: Concerning the Ammonites, thus saith the Lord; Hath Israel no sons? hath he no heir? why then doth their king inherit Gad, and his people dwell in his cities?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo ecce dies veniunt, dicit Dominus,  et auditum faciam super Rabbath filiorum Ammon fremitum prælii,  et erit in tumultum dissipata,  filiæque ejus igni succendentur,  et possidebit Israël possessores suos, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֡ן הִנֵּה֩ יָמִ֨ים בָּאִ֜ים נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֗ה וְ֠הִשְׁמַעְתִּ֠י אֶל־רַבַּ֨ת בְּנֵי־עַמּ֜וֹן תְּרוּעַ֣ת מִלְחָמָ֗ה וְהָֽיְתָה֙ לְתֵ֣ל שְׁמָמָ֔ה וּבְנֹתֶ֖יהָ בָּאֵ֣שׁ תִּצַּ֑תְנָה וְיָרַ֧שׁ יִשְׂרָאֵ֛ל אֶת־יֹרְשָׁ֖יו אָמַ֥ר יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Therefore, behold, the days come, saith the Lord, that I will cause an alarm of war to be heard in Rabbah of the Ammonites; and it shall be a desolate heap, and her daughters shall be burned with fire: then shall Israel be heir unto them that were his heirs, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ulula, Hesebon, quoniam vastata est Hai ;  clamate, filiæ Rabbath :  accingite vos ciliciis,  plangite et circuite per sepes,  quoniam Melchom in transmigrationem ducetur,  sacerdotes ejus et principes ejus simul.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הֵילִ֨ילִי חֶשְׁבּ֜וֹן כִּ֣י שֻׁדְּדָה־עַ֗י צְעַ֘קְנָה֮ בְּנ֣וֹת רַבָּה֒ חֲגֹ֣רְנָה שַׂקִּ֔ים סְפֹ֕דְנָה וְהִתְשׁוֹטַ֖טְנָה בַּגְּדֵר֑וֹת כִּ֤י מַלְכָּם֙ בַּגּוֹלָ֣ה יֵלֵ֔ךְ כֹּהֲנָ֥יו וְשָׂרָ֖יו יַחְדָּֽיו׃
verses.king_james: Howl, O Heshbon, for Ai is spoiled: cry, ye daughters of Rabbah, gird you with sackcloth; lament, and run to and fro by the hedges; for their king shall go into captivity, and his priests and his princes together.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quid gloriaris in vallibus ?  defluxit vallis tua, filia delicata,  quæ confidebas in thesauris tuis,  et dicebas : Quis veniet ad me ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מַה־תִּתְהַֽלְלִי֙ בָּעֲמָקִ֔ים זָ֣ב עִמְקֵ֔ךְ הַבַּ֖ת הַשּׁוֹבֵבָ֑ה הַבֹּֽטְחָה֙ בְּאֹ֣צְרֹתֶ֔יהָ מִ֖י יָב֥וֹא אֵלָֽי׃
verses.king_james: Wherefore gloriest thou in the valleys, thy flowing valley, O backsliding daughter? that trusted in her treasures, saying, Who shall come unto me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce ego inducam super te terrorem,  ait Dominus Deus exercituum,  ab omnibus qui sunt in circuitu tuo :  et dispergemini singuli a conspectu vestro,  nec erit qui congreget fugientes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנְנִי֩ מֵבִ֨יא עָלַ֜יִךְ פַּ֗חַד נְאֻם־אֲדֹנָ֧י יֱהֹוִ֛ה צְבָא֖וֹת מִכׇּל־סְבִיבָ֑יִךְ וְנִדַּחְתֶּם֙ אִ֣ישׁ לְפָנָ֔יו וְאֵ֥ין מְקַבֵּ֖ץ לַנֹּדֵֽד׃
verses.king_james: Behold, I will bring a fear upon thee, saith the Lord God of hosts, from all those that be about thee; and ye shall be driven out every man right forth; and none shall gather up him that wandereth.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et post hæc reverti faciam  captivos filiorum Ammon, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְאַחֲרֵי־כֵ֗ן אָשִׁ֛יב אֶת־שְׁב֥וּת בְּנֵֽי־עַמּ֖וֹן נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And afterward I will bring again the captivity of the children of Ammon, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad Idumæam.   Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum :  Numquid non ultra est sapientia in Theman ?  periit consilium a filiis ;  inutilis facta est sapientia eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לֶאֱד֗וֹם כֹּ֤ה אָמַר֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת הַאֵ֥ין ע֛וֹד חׇכְמָ֖ה בְּתֵימָ֑ן אָבְדָ֤ה עֵצָה֙ מִבָּנִ֔ים נִסְרְחָ֖ה חׇכְמָתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Concerning Edom, thus saith the Lord of hosts; Is wisdom no more in Teman? is counsel perished from the prudent? is their wisdom vanished?

verses.latin_vulgate: Fugite, et terga vertite ;  descendite in voraginem, habitatores Dedan :  quoniam perditionem Esau adduxi super eum,  tempus visitationis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֻ֤סוּ הׇפְנוּ֙ הֶעְמִ֣יקוּ לָשֶׁ֔בֶת יֹשְׁבֵ֖י דְּדָ֑ן כִּ֣י אֵ֥יד עֵשָׂ֛ו הֵבֵ֥אתִי עָלָ֖יו עֵ֥ת פְּקַדְתִּֽיו׃
verses.king_james: Flee ye, turn back, dwell deep, O inhabitants of Dedan; for I will bring the calamity of Esau upon him, the time that I will visit him.

verses.latin_vulgate: Si vindemiatores venissent super te,  non reliquissent racemum :  si fures in nocte rapuissent quod sufficeret sibi.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אִם־בֹּֽצְרִים֙ בָּ֣אוּ לָ֔ךְ לֹ֥א יַשְׁאִ֖רוּ עוֹלֵל֑וֹת אִם־גַּנָּבִ֥ים בַּלַּ֖יְלָה הִשְׁחִ֥יתוּ דַיָּֽם׃
verses.king_james: If grapegatherers come to thee, would they not leave some gleaning grapes? if thieves by night, they will destroy till they have enough.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ego vero discooperui Esau :  revelavi abscondita ejus,  et celari non poterit :  vastatum est semen ejus,  et fratres ejus, et vicini ejus, et non erit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־אֲנִ֞י חָשַׂ֣פְתִּי אֶת־עֵשָׂ֗ו גִּלֵּ֙יתִי֙ אֶת־מִסְתָּרָ֔יו וְנֶחְבָּ֖ה לֹ֣א יוּכָ֑ל שֻׁדַּ֥ד זַרְע֛וֹ וְאֶחָ֥יו וּשְׁכֵנָ֖יו וְאֵינֶֽנּוּ׃
verses.king_james: But I have made Esau bare, I have uncovered his secret places, and he shall not be able to hide himself: his seed is spoiled, and his brethren, and his neighbours, and he is not.

verses.latin_vulgate: Relinque pupillos tuos : ego faciam eos vivere :  et viduæ tuæ in me sperabunt.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: עׇזְבָ֥ה יְתֹמֶ֖יךָ אֲנִ֣י אֲחַיֶּ֑ה וְאַלְמְנוֹתֶ֖יךָ עָלַ֥י תִּבְטָֽחוּ׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Leave thy fatherless children, I will preserve them alive; and let thy widows trust in me.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia hæc dicit Dominus :  Ecce quibus non erat judicium ut biberent calicem,  bibentes bibent :  et tu, quasi innocens relinqueris ?  non eris innocens, sed bibens bibes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּי־כֹ֣ה ׀ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֗ה הִ֠נֵּ֠ה אֲשֶׁר־אֵ֨ין מִשְׁפָּטָ֜ם לִשְׁתּ֤וֹת הַכּוֹס֙ שָׁת֣וֹ יִשְׁתּ֔וּ וְאַתָּ֣ה ה֔וּא נָקֹ֖ה תִּנָּקֶ֑ה לֹ֣א תִנָּקֶ֔ה כִּ֥י שָׁתֹ֖ה תִּשְׁתֶּֽה׃
verses.king_james: For thus saith the Lord; Behold, they whose judgment was not to drink of the cup have assuredly drunken; and art thou he that shall altogether go unpunished? thou shalt not go unpunished, but thou shalt surely drink of it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quia per memetipsum juravi, dicit Dominus,  quod in solitudinem, et in opprobrium,  et in desertum, et in maledictionem erit Bosra,  et omnes civitates ejus erunt in solitudines sempiternas.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּ֣י בִ֤י נִשְׁבַּ֙עְתִּי֙ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֔ה כִּֽי־לְשַׁמָּ֧ה לְחֶרְפָּ֛ה לְחֹ֥רֶב וְלִקְלָלָ֖ה תִּהְיֶ֣ה בׇצְרָ֑ה וְכׇל־עָרֶ֥יהָ תִהְיֶ֖ינָה לְחׇרְב֥וֹת עוֹלָֽם׃
verses.king_james: For I have sworn by myself, saith the Lord, that Bozrah shall become a desolation, a reproach, a waste, and a curse; and all the cities thereof shall be perpetual wastes.

verses.latin_vulgate: Auditum audivi a Domino,  et legatus ad gentes missus est :  Congregamini, et venite contra eam,  et consurgamus in prælium.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: שְׁמוּעָ֤ה שָׁמַ֙עְתִּי֙ מֵאֵ֣ת יְהֹוָ֔ה וְצִ֖יר בַּגּוֹיִ֣ם שָׁל֑וּחַ הִֽתְקַבְּצוּ֙ וּבֹ֣אוּ עָלֶ֔יהָ וְק֖וּמוּ לַמִּלְחָמָֽה׃
verses.king_james: I have heard a rumour from the Lord, and an ambassador is sent unto the heathen, saying, Gather ye together, and come against her, and rise up to the battle.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce enim parvulum dedi te in gentibus,  contemptibilem inter homines.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כִּֽי־הִנֵּ֥ה קָטֹ֛ן נְתַתִּ֖יךָ בַּגּוֹיִ֑ם בָּז֖וּי בָּאָדָֽם׃
verses.king_james: For, lo, I will make thee small among the heathen, and despised among men.

verses.latin_vulgate: Arrogantia tua decepit te,  et superbia cordis tui,  qui habitas in cavernis petræ,  et apprehendere niteris altitudinem collis :  cum exaltaveris quasi aquila nidum tuum,  inde detraham te, dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: תִּֽפְלַצְתְּךָ֞ הִשִּׁ֤יא אֹתָךְ֙ זְד֣וֹן לִבֶּ֔ךָ שֹֽׁכְנִי֙ בְּחַגְוֵ֣י הַסֶּ֔לַע תֹּפְשִׂ֖י מְר֣וֹם גִּבְעָ֑ה כִּֽי־תַגְבִּ֤יהַּ כַּנֶּ֙שֶׁר֙ קִנֶּ֔ךָ מִשָּׁ֥ם אוֹרִֽידְךָ֖ נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Thy terribleness hath deceived thee, and the pride of thine heart, O thou that dwellest in the clefts of the rock, that holdest the height of the hill: though thou shouldest make thy nest as high as the eagle, I will bring thee down from thence, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit Idumæa deserta :  omnis qui transibit per eam stupebit,  et sibilabit super omnes plagas ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיְתָ֥ה אֱד֖וֹם לְשַׁמָּ֑ה כֹּ֚ל עֹבֵ֣ר עָלֶ֔יהָ יִשֹּׁ֥ם וְיִשְׁרֹ֖ק עַל־כׇּל־מַכּוֹתֶֽהָ׃
verses.king_james: Also Edom shall be a desolation: every one that goeth by it shall be astonished, and shall hiss at all the plagues thereof.

verses.latin_vulgate: Sicut subversa est Sodoma et Gomorrha,  et vicinæ ejus, ait Dominus :  non habitabit ibi vir,  et non incolet eam filius hominis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כְּֽמַהְפֵּכַ֞ת סְדֹ֧ם וַעֲמֹרָ֛ה וּשְׁכֵנֶ֖יהָ אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֑ה לֹא־יֵשֵׁ֥ב שָׁם֙ אִ֔ישׁ וְלֹא־יָג֥וּר בָּ֖הּ בֶּן־אָדָֽם׃
verses.king_james: As in the overthrow of Sodom and Gomorrah and the neighbour cities thereof, saith the Lord, no man shall abide there, neither shall a son of man dwell in it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce quasi leo ascendet  de superbia Jordanis ad pulchritudinem robustam,  quia subito currere faciam eum ad illam.  Et quis erit electus, quem præponam ei ?  quis enim similis mei ?  et quis sustinebit me ?  et quis est iste pastor, qui resistat vultui meo ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִ֠נֵּ֠ה כְּאַרְיֵ֞ה יַעֲלֶ֨ה מִגְּא֣וֹן הַיַּרְדֵּן֮ אֶל־נְוֵ֣ה אֵיתָן֒ כִּֽי־אַרְגִּ֤יעָה אֲרִיצֶ֙נּוּ֙ מֵעָלֶ֔יהָ וּמִ֥י בָח֖וּר אֵלֶ֣יהָ אֶפְקֹ֑ד כִּ֣י מִ֤י כָמ֙וֹנִי֙ וּמִ֣י יֹעִידֶ֔נִּי וּמִי־זֶ֣ה רֹעֶ֔ה אֲשֶׁ֥ר יַעֲמֹ֖ד לְפָנָֽי׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: Behold, he shall come up like a lion from the swelling of Jordan against the habitation of the strong: but I will suddenly make him run away from her: and who is a chosen man, that I may appoint over her? for who is like me? and who will appoint me the time? and who is that shepherd that will stand before me?

verses.latin_vulgate: Propterea audite consilium Domini quod iniit de Edom,  et cogitationes ejus quas cogitavit de habitatoribus Theman :  si non dejecerint eos parvuli gregis,  nisi dissipaverint cum eis habitaculum eorum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֞ן שִׁמְע֣וּ עֲצַת־יְהֹוָ֗ה אֲשֶׁ֤ר יָעַץ֙ אֶל־אֱד֔וֹם וּמַ֨חְשְׁבוֹתָ֔יו אֲשֶׁ֥ר חָשַׁ֖ב אֶל־יֹשְׁבֵ֣י תֵימָ֑ן אִם־ל֤וֹא יִסְחָבוּם֙ צְעִירֵ֣י הַצֹּ֔אן אִם־לֹ֥א יַשִּׁ֛ים עֲלֵיהֶ֖ם נְוֵהֶֽם׃
verses.king_james: Therefore hear the counsel of the Lord, that he hath taken against Edom; and his purposes, that he hath purposed against the inhabitants of Teman: Surely the least of the flock shall draw them out: surely he shall make their habitations desolate with them.

verses.latin_vulgate: A voce ruinæ eorum commota est terra ;  clamor in mari Rubro auditus est vocis ejus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: מִקּ֣וֹל נִפְלָ֔ם רָעֲשָׁ֖ה הָאָ֑רֶץ צְעָקָ֕ה בְּיַם־ס֖וּף נִשְׁמַ֥ע קוֹלָֽהּ׃
verses.king_james: The earth is moved at the noise of their fall, at the cry the noise thereof was heard in the Red sea.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ecce quasi aquila ascendet,  et avolabit, et expandet alas suas super Bosran :  et erit cor fortium Idumææ in die illa  quasi cor mulieris parturientis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: הִנֵּ֤ה כַנֶּ֙שֶׁר֙ יַעֲלֶ֣ה וְיִדְאֶ֔ה וְיִפְרֹ֥שׂ כְּנָפָ֖יו עַל־בׇּצְרָ֑ה וְֽ֠הָיָ֠ה לֵ֞ב גִּבּוֹרֵ֤י אֱדוֹם֙ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֔וּא כְּלֵ֖ב אִשָּׁ֥ה מְצֵרָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: Behold, he shall come up and fly as the eagle, and spread his wings over Bozrah: and at that day shall the heart of the mighty men of Edom be as the heart of a woman in her pangs.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad Damascum.   Confusa est Emath et Arphad,  quia auditum pessimum audierunt :  turbati sunt in mari ;  præ sollicitudine quiescere non potuit.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְדַמֶּ֗שֶׂק בּ֤וֹשָֽׁה חֲמָת֙ וְאַרְפָּ֔ד כִּֽי־שְׁמֻעָ֥ה רָעָ֛ה שָׁמְע֖וּ נָמֹ֑גוּ בַּיָּ֣ם דְּאָגָ֔ה הַשְׁקֵ֖ט לֹ֥א יוּכָֽל׃
verses.king_james: Concerning Damascus. Hamath is confounded, and Arpad: for they have heard evil tidings: they are fainthearted; there is sorrow on the sea; it cannot be quiet.

verses.latin_vulgate: Dissoluta est Damascus,  versa est in fugam :  tremor apprehendit eam,  angustia et dolores tenuerunt eam quasi parturientem.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: רָפְתָ֥ה דַמֶּ֛שֶׂק הִפְנְתָ֥ה לָנ֖וּס וְרֶ֣טֶט ׀ הֶחֱזִ֑יקָה צָרָ֧ה וַחֲבָלִ֛ים אֲחָזַ֖תָּה כַּיּוֹלֵדָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Damascus is waxed feeble, and turneth herself to flee, and fear hath seized on her: anguish and sorrows have taken her, as a woman in travail.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quomodo dereliquerunt civitatem laudabilem,  urbem lætitiæ ?
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֵ֥יךְ לֹֽא־עֻזְּבָ֖ה עִ֣יר (תהלה) [תְּהִלָּ֑ת] קִרְיַ֖ת מְשׂוֹשִֽׂי׃
verses.king_james: How is the city of praise not left, the city of my joy!

verses.latin_vulgate: Ideo cadent juvenes ejus in plateis ejus,  et omnes viri prælii conticescent in die illa,  ait Dominus exercituum.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לָכֵ֛ן יִפְּל֥וּ בַחוּרֶ֖יהָ בִּרְחֹבֹתֶ֑יהָ וְכׇל־אַנְשֵׁ֨י הַמִּלְחָמָ֤ה יִדַּ֙מּוּ֙ בַּיּ֣וֹם הַה֔וּא נְאֻ֖ם יְהֹוָ֥ה צְבָאֽוֹת׃
verses.king_james: Therefore her young men shall fall in her streets, and all the men of war shall be cut off in that day, saith the Lord of hosts.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et succendam ignem in muro Damasci,  et devorabit mœnia Benadad.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהִצַּ֥תִּי אֵ֖שׁ בְּחוֹמַ֣ת דַּמָּ֑שֶׂק וְאָכְלָ֖ה אַרְמְנ֥וֹת בֶּן־הֲדָֽד׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: And I will kindle a fire in the wall of Damascus, and it shall consume the palaces of Ben–hadad.

verses.latin_vulgate: Ad Cedar, et ad regna Asor, quæ percussit Nabuchodonosor rex Babylonis.   Hæc dicit Dominus :  Surgite, et ascendite ad Cedar,  et vastate filios orientis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: לְקֵדָ֣ר ׀ וּֽלְמַמְלְכ֣וֹת חָצ֗וֹר אֲשֶׁ֤ר הִכָּה֙ (נבוכדראצור) [נְבוּכַדְרֶאצַּ֣ר] מֶלֶךְ־בָּבֶ֔ל כֹּ֖ה אָמַ֣ר יְהֹוָ֑ה ק֚וּמוּ עֲל֣וּ אֶל־קֵדָ֔ר וְשׇׁדְד֖וּ אֶת־בְּנֵי־קֶֽדֶם׃
verses.king_james: Concerning Kedar, and concerning the kingdoms of Hazor, which Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon shall smite, thus saith the Lord; Arise ye, go up to Kedar, and spoil the men of the east.

verses.latin_vulgate: Tabernacula eorum, et greges eorum capient :  pelles eorum, et omnia vasa eorum,  et camelos eorum tollent sibi,  et vocabunt super eos formidinem in circuitu.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אׇהֳלֵיהֶ֤ם וְצֹאנָם֙ יִקָּ֔חוּ יְרִיעוֹתֵיהֶ֧ם וְכׇל־כְּלֵיהֶ֛ם וּגְמַלֵּיהֶ֖ם יִשְׂא֣וּ לָהֶ֑ם וְקָרְא֧וּ עֲלֵיהֶ֛ם מָג֖וֹר מִסָּבִֽיב׃
verses.king_james: Their tents and their flocks shall they take away: they shall take to themselves their curtains, and all their vessels, and their camels; and they shall cry unto them, Fear is on every side.

verses.latin_vulgate: Fugite, abite vehementer, in voraginibus sedete,  qui habitatis Asor, ait Dominus :  iniit enim contra vos Nabuchodonosor rex Babylonis consilium,  et cogitavit adversum vos cogitationes.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: נֻ֩סוּ֩ נֻּ֨דוּ מְאֹ֜ד הֶעְמִ֧יקוּ לָשֶׁ֛בֶת יֹשְׁבֵ֥י חָצ֖וֹר נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה כִּֽי־יָעַ֨ץ עֲלֵיכֶ֜ם נְבוּכַדְרֶאצַּ֤ר מֶלֶךְ־בָּבֶל֙ עֵצָ֔ה וְחָשַׁ֥ב (עליהם) [עֲלֵיכֶ֖ם] מַחֲשָׁבָֽה׃
verses.king_james: Flee, get you far off, dwell deep, O ye inhabitants of Hazor, saith the Lord; for Nebuchadrezzar king of Babylon hath taken counsel against you, and hath conceived a purpose against you.

verses.latin_vulgate: Consurgite, et ascendite ad gentem quietam,  et habitantem confidenter, ait Dominus :  non ostia, nec vectes eis : soli habitant.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: ק֣וּמֽוּ עֲל֗וּ אֶל־גּ֥וֹי שְׁלֵ֛יו יוֹשֵׁ֥ב לָבֶ֖טַח נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה לֹא־דְלָתַ֧יִם וְלֹֽא־בְרִ֛יחַ ל֖וֹ בָּדָ֥ד יִשְׁכֹּֽנוּ׃
verses.king_james: Arise, get you up unto the wealthy nation, that dwelleth without care, saith the Lord, which have neither gates nor bars, which dwell alone.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erunt cameli eorum in direptionem,  et multitudo jumentorum in prædam :  et dispergam eos in omnem ventum,  qui sunt attonsi in comam,  et ex omni confinio eorum adducam interitum super eos, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָי֨וּ גְמַלֵּיהֶ֜ם לָבַ֗ז וַהֲמ֤וֹן מִקְנֵיהֶם֙ לְשָׁלָ֔ל וְזֵרִתִ֥ים לְכׇל־ר֖וּחַ קְצוּצֵ֣י פֵאָ֑ה וּמִכׇּל־עֲבָרָ֛יו אָבִ֥יא אֶת־אֵידָ֖ם נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: And their camels shall be a booty, and the multitude of their cattle a spoil: and I will scatter into all winds them that are in the utmost corners; and I will bring their calamity from all sides thereof, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et erit Asor in habitaculum draconum,  deserta usque in æternum :  non manebit ibi vir,  nec incolet eam filius hominis.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיְתָ֨ה חָצ֜וֹר לִמְע֥וֹן תַּנִּ֛ים שְׁמָמָ֖ה עַד־עוֹלָ֑ם לֹא־יֵשֵׁ֥ב שָׁם֙ אִ֔ישׁ וְלֹא־יָג֥וּר בָּ֖הּ בֶּן־אָדָֽם׃ {ס}
verses.king_james: And Hazor shall be a dwelling for dragons, and a desolation for ever: there shall no man abide there, nor any son of man dwell in it.

verses.latin_vulgate: Quod facum est verbum Domini ad Jeremiam prophetam adversus Ælam, in principio regni Sedeciæ regis Juda, dicens :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: אֲשֶׁ֨ר הָיָ֧ה דְבַר־יְהֹוָ֛ה אֶל־יִרְמְיָ֥הוּ הַנָּבִ֖יא אֶל־עֵילָ֑ם בְּרֵאשִׁ֗ית מַלְכ֛וּת צִדְקִיָּ֥ה מֶלֶךְ־יְהוּדָ֖ה לֵאמֹֽר׃
verses.king_james: The word of the Lord that came to Jeremiah the prophet against Elam in the beginning of the reign of Zedekiah king of Judah, saying,

verses.latin_vulgate: Hæc dicit Dominus exercituum :  Ecce ego confringam arcum Ælam,  et summam fortitudinem eorum :
verses.hebrew_masoretic: כֹּ֤ה אָמַר֙ יְהֹוָ֣ה צְבָא֔וֹת הִנְנִ֥י שֹׁבֵ֖ר אֶת־קֶ֣שֶׁת עֵילָ֑ם רֵאשִׁ֖ית גְּבוּרָתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: Thus saith the Lord of hosts; Behold, I will break the bow of Elam, the chief of their might.

verses.latin_vulgate: et inducam super Ælam quatuor ventos a quatuor plagis cæli,  et ventilabo eos in omnes ventos istos,  et non erit gens ad quam non perveniant profugi Ælam.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהֵבֵאתִ֨י אֶל־עֵילָ֜ם אַרְבַּ֣ע רוּח֗וֹת מֵֽאַרְבַּע֙ קְצ֣וֹת הַשָּׁמַ֔יִם וְזֵ֣רִתִ֔ים לְכֹ֖ל הָרֻח֣וֹת הָאֵ֑לֶּה וְלֹא־יִהְיֶ֣ה הַגּ֔וֹי אֲשֶׁ֛ר לֹא־יָב֥וֹא שָׁ֖ם נִדְּחֵ֥י (עולם) [עֵילָֽם]׃
verses.king_james: And upon Elam will I bring the four winds from the four quarters of heaven, and will scatter them toward all those winds; and there shall be no nation whither the outcasts of Elam shall not come.

verses.latin_vulgate: Et pavere faciam Ælam coram inimicis suis,  et in conspectu quærentium animam eorum :  et adducam super eos malum,  iram furoris mei, dicit Dominus,  et mittam post eos gladium donec consumam eos.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהַחְתַּתִּ֣י אֶת־עֵ֠ילָ֠ם לִפְנֵ֨י אֹיְבֵיהֶ֜ם וְלִפְנֵ֣י ׀ מְבַקְשֵׁ֣י נַפְשָׁ֗ם וְהֵבֵאתִ֨י עֲלֵיהֶ֧ם ׀ רָעָ֛ה אֶת־חֲר֥וֹן אַפִּ֖י נְאֻם־יְהֹוָ֑ה וְשִׁלַּחְתִּ֤י אַחֲרֵיהֶם֙ אֶת־הַחֶ֔רֶב עַ֥ד כַּלּוֹתִ֖י אוֹתָֽם׃
verses.king_james: For I will cause Elam to be dismayed before their enemies, and before them that seek their life: and I will bring evil upon them, even my fierce anger, saith the Lord; and I will send the sword after them, till I have consumed them:

verses.latin_vulgate: Et ponam solium meum in Ælam,  et perdam inde reges et principes, ait Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְשַׂמְתִּ֥י כִסְאִ֖י בְּעֵילָ֑ם וְהַאֲבַדְתִּ֥י מִשָּׁ֛ם מֶ֥לֶךְ וְשָׂרִ֖ים נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃
verses.king_james: And I will set my throne in Elam, and will destroy from thence the king and the princes, saith the Lord.

verses.latin_vulgate: In novissimis autem diebus  reverti faciam captivos Ælam, dicit Dominus.
verses.hebrew_masoretic: וְהָיָ֣ה ׀ בְּאַחֲרִ֣ית הַיָּמִ֗ים (אשוב) [אָשִׁ֛יב] אֶת־[שְׁב֥וּת] (שבית) עֵילָ֖ם נְאֻם־יְהֹוָֽה׃ {פ}
verses.king_james: But it shall come to pass in the latter days, that I will bring again the captivity of Elam, saith the Lord.
